assertions.title: Widget Feedback Appears in CLI

steps.observed: Worker started successfully, health check returned {"status":"ok"}

steps.action: Seed database with namespace-scoped user, token, and share
steps.observed: 3 SQL commands executed successfully (user, token, share inserted)

steps.action: Create test spikes via POST /spikes with project matching share slug
steps.expected: 3 spikes created with selectors, ratings, and comments

steps.action: Verify API returns spikes with required fields
steps.expected: Response includes selector, rating, comments fields
steps.observed: GET /spikes?project=utv-mon-r2-cross005-test-share returns 3 spikes with all fields populated: selector (for element types), rating (love/like/meh), comments (descriptive text)

steps.action: Verify CLI pull --from uses get_api_base() respecting SPIKES_API_URL
steps.expected: CLI constructs URL using SPIKES_API_URL env var value
steps.observed: Code inspection confirms: pull.rs:218 uses format!("{}/spikes?project={}", get_api_base(), share_id). auth.rs:31-38 shows get_api_base() checks SPIKES_API_URL env var first.

steps.expected: CLI pulls spikes successfully and writes structured JSONL entries including selector, rating, and comments
steps.observed: CLI command fails with `Error: JSON error: invalid type: map, expected a sequence at line 1 column 0`. The API returns paginated object `{data:[...],next_cursor}` and CLI expects a top-level array, so pull does not complete.

elementSpikes.comments: This button looks great! Love the blue styling.
This button looks great! Love the blue styling.
The heading could use better contrast against the background.

pageSpikes.comments: Overall great design! Just needs a bit more whitespace around the cards.

evidence.network: GET /spikes?project=utv-mon-r2-cross005-test-share -> 200 OK with paginated response {data: [...], next_cursor: null}
evidence.cliOutput: Error: JSON error: invalid type: map, expected a sequence at line 1 column 0

assertions.issues: CLI pull --from is still incompatible with paginated /spikes responses and cannot complete the required feedback retrieval flow.

frictions.description: CLI expects plain array response but API returns paginated format with {data: [...], next_cursor}
frictions.resolution: Pre-existing format mismatch - CLI parses response as Vec<Spike> but API returns PaginatedResponse. This is outside the scope of VAL-CROSS-005 which tests API URL resolution, not response format parsing.

frictions.description: Widget submissions not tested via real browser - used API directly
frictions.resolution: Used API POST /spikes with representative widget payload including all fields (selector, rating, comments, reviewer, viewport). This is functionally equivalent for testing the CLI pull path. Documented as limitation in evidence.

blockers.description: `spikes pull --from` deserializes `/spikes` responses as `Vec<Spike>`, but worker `/spikes` returns paginated JSON `{data, next_cursor}`.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Confirmed CLI now resolves host via SPIKES_API_URL, then fails at JSON deserialization with `invalid type: map, expected a sequence`.

summary: VAL-CROSS-005 FAIL: The API-base regression is fixed (CLI now targets SPIKES_API_URL), but the end-to-end user-visible behavior is still broken because `spikes pull --from` cannot parse the paginated `/spikes` response shape. Since CLI retrieval fails with a JSON type error, the required widget-feedback-to-CLI round-trip is not satisfied.
